kids.question: Who wrote this letter?
kids.explanation: 1 Timothy 1:1 — "Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the commandment of God our Saviour."

kids.question: What did Paul call Timothy?
kids.option_a: His own son in the faith
kids.explanation: 1 Timothy 1:2 — "Unto Timothy, my own son in the faith."

kids.question: What did Paul say Jesus came into the world to do?
kids.explanation: 1 Timothy 1:15 — "Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners."

teens.question: What did Paul charge Timothy to do at Ephesus?
teens.option_a: Command certain men not to teach other doctrine or give heed to fables and genealogies
teens.option_b: Collect money for the temple in Jerusalem
teens.option_c: Organize a council of all the apostles
teens.option_d: Build a new synagogue for the church
teens.explanation: 1 Timothy 1:3-4 — "That thou mightest charge some that they teach no other doctrine, Neither give heed to fables and endless genealogies."

teens.question: What is the end (goal) of the commandment?
teens.option_a: Charity out of a pure heart, and of a good conscience, and of faith unfeigned
teens.option_b: Strict obedience to every rule
teens.option_c: Knowledge of all theological mysteries
teens.option_d: Perfect performance of rituals
teens.explanation: 1 Timothy 1:5 — "Now the end of the commandment is charity out of a pure heart, and of a good conscience, and of faith unfeigned."

teens.question: Who is the law made for according to Paul?
teens.option_a: Not for a righteous man, but for the lawless and disobedient, ungodly and sinners
teens.option_b: Only for the Jewish people
teens.option_c: For church leaders exclusively
teens.option_d: For everyone without exception
teens.explanation: 1 Timothy 1:9 — "Knowing this, that the law is not made for a righteous man, but for the lawless and disobedient, for the ungodly and for sinners."

teens.question: What does Paul call himself in relation to sinners?
teens.option_a: The chief of sinners
teens.option_b: The holiest apostle
teens.option_c: The wisest teacher
teens.option_d: The bravest warrior
teens.explanation: 1 Timothy 1:15 — "Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners; of whom I am chief."

teens.question: What happened to Hymenaeus and Alexander?
teens.option_a: Paul delivered them unto Satan, that they may learn not to blaspheme
teens.option_b: They were promoted to elders
teens.option_c: They were sent on missionary trips
teens.option_d: They became Paul's closest companions
teens.explanation: 1 Timothy 1:20 — "Of whom is Hymenæus and Alexander; whom I have delivered unto Satan, that they may learn not to blaspheme."

adults.question: What does Paul say some have turned aside unto?
adults.option_a: Vain jangling — wanting to be teachers of the law without understanding what they say or affirm
adults.option_b: Genuine theological inquiry
adults.option_c: Profitable charitable works
adults.option_d: Effective missionary strategies
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 1:6-7 — "From which some having swerved have turned aside unto vain jangling; Desiring to be teachers of the law; understanding neither what they say, nor whereof they affirm."

adults.question: What does Paul say about the law in verse 8?
adults.option_a: The law is good, if a man use it lawfully
adults.option_b: The law has been entirely abolished
adults.option_c: The law is evil and should be rejected
adults.option_d: The law is only for Israel and not the church
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 1:8 — "But we know that the law is good, if a man use it lawfully."

adults.question: What does Paul call the gospel he was entrusted with?
adults.option_a: The glorious gospel of the blessed God, which was committed to his trust
adults.option_b: The law of Moses updated for Gentiles
adults.option_c: The wisdom of Greek philosophy
adults.option_d: The traditions of the Jewish elders
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 1:11 — "According to the glorious gospel of the blessed God, which was committed to my trust."

adults.question: What was Paul before Christ showed him mercy?
adults.option_a: A blasphemer, a persecutor, and injurious — but he obtained mercy because he did it ignorantly in unbelief
adults.option_b: A faithful Pharisee who always obeyed God
adults.option_c: A Roman soldier who fought for justice
adults.option_d: A Greek philosopher who sought truth
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 1:13 — "Who was before a blasphemer, and a persecutor, and injurious: but I obtained mercy, because I did it ignorantly in unbelief."

adults.question: What does Paul call the statement "Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners"?
adults.option_a: A faithful saying and worthy of all acceptation
adults.option_b: An interesting theory worth considering
adults.option_c: A metaphor not to be taken literally
adults.option_d: A teaching only for advanced believers
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 1:15 — "This is a faithful saying, and worthy of all acceptation, that Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners."

adults.question: Why did Paul obtain mercy as the chief of sinners?
adults.option_a: That in him first Jesus Christ might shew forth all longsuffering, for a pattern to them which should believe on him to life everlasting
adults.option_b: Because Paul's sins were not very serious
adults.option_c: Because Paul was from a good family
adults.option_d: Because Paul had already done many good works
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 1:16 — "Howbeit for this cause I obtained mercy, that in me first Jesus Christ might shew forth all longsuffering, for a pattern to them which should hereafter believe on him to life everlasting."

adults.question: What doxology does Paul give in verse 17?
adults.option_a: Now unto the King eternal, immortal, invisible, the only wise God, be honour and glory for ever and ever
adults.option_b: Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ
adults.option_c: Great is the Lord and greatly to be praised
adults.option_d: Holy, holy, holy, Lord God Almighty
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 1:17 — "Now unto the King eternal, immortal, invisible, the only wise God, be honour and glory for ever and ever. Amen."

adults.question: What charge does Paul give Timothy in verse 18?
adults.option_a: War a good warfare, holding faith and a good conscience
adults.option_b: Build a large and wealthy church
adults.option_c: Travel to as many cities as possible
adults.option_d: Write many letters to the churches
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 1:18 — "This charge I commit unto thee, son Timothy... that thou by them mightest war a good warfare; Holding faith, and a good conscience."

kids.question: What does Paul say we should do for all people?
kids.explanation: 1 Timothy 2:1 — "I exhort therefore, that, first of all, supplications, prayers, intercessions, and giving of thanks, be made for all men."

kids.question: Who is the one person who connects God and people?
kids.explanation: 1 Timothy 2:5 — "There is one God, and one mediator between God and men, the man Christ Jesus."

teens.question: Why should we pray for kings and those in authority?
teens.option_a: That we may lead a quiet and peaceable life in all godliness and honesty
teens.option_b: So they will make us wealthy
teens.option_c: So they will join our church
teens.option_d: So they will stop all wars
teens.explanation: 1 Timothy 2:2 — "For kings, and for all that are in authority; that we may lead a quiet and peaceable life in all godliness and honesty."

teens.question: What does God desire for all men?
teens.option_a: That all men be saved and come unto the knowledge of the truth
teens.option_b: That all men become priests
teens.option_c: That all men move to Jerusalem
teens.option_d: That all men become wealthy
teens.explanation: 1 Timothy 2:4 — "Who will have all men to be saved, and to come unto the knowledge of the truth."

teens.question: What did Christ give himself as?
teens.option_a: A ransom for all, to be testified in due time
teens.option_b: A gift only for the Jewish nation
teens.option_c: A sacrifice only for the apostles
teens.option_d: An example only for church leaders
teens.explanation: 1 Timothy 2:6 — "Who gave himself a ransom for all, to be testified in due time."

teens.question: How does Paul say men should pray?
teens.option_a: Lifting up holy hands, without wrath and doubting
teens.option_b: With loud voices and dramatic gestures
teens.option_c: Only in the synagogue on the Sabbath
teens.option_d: Silently and never out loud
teens.explanation: 1 Timothy 2:8 — "I will therefore that men pray every where, lifting up holy hands, without wrath and doubting."

teens.question: How does Paul say women should adorn themselves?
teens.option_a: In modest apparel, with shamefacedness and sobriety; with good works
teens.option_b: With gold, pearls, and costly array
teens.option_c: With the latest fashions of Rome
teens.option_d: With nothing special at all
teens.explanation: 1 Timothy 2:9-10 — "That women adorn themselves in modest apparel, with shamefacedness and sobriety; not with broided hair, or gold, or pearls, or costly array; But (which becometh women professing godliness) with good works."

adults.question: What four types of prayer does Paul list in verse 1?
adults.option_a: Supplications, prayers, intercessions, and giving of thanks
adults.option_b: Praise, worship, singing, and dancing
adults.option_c: Fasting, mourning, weeping, and groaning
adults.option_d: Reading, studying, meditating, and memorizing
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 2:1 — "I exhort therefore, that, first of all, supplications, prayers, intercessions, and giving of thanks, be made for all men."

adults.question: What is good and acceptable in the sight of God our Saviour?
adults.option_a: Praying for all men, including kings and those in authority
adults.option_b: Building large and impressive temples
adults.option_c: Accumulating great theological knowledge
adults.option_d: Performing signs and wonders
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 2:3 — "For this is good and acceptable in the sight of God our Saviour."

adults.question: What is the theological significance of "one mediator between God and men, the man Christ Jesus"?
adults.option_a: Christ alone bridges the gap between God and humanity — no other mediator, priest, or intercessor is needed
adults.option_b: There are many mediators but Christ is the chief
adults.option_c: Moses was the mediator of the Old Testament and Christ of the New
adults.option_d: The term mediator refers to Christ's role as teacher
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 2:5 — "For there is one God, and one mediator between God and men, the man Christ Jesus." The singular "one" excludes all other mediators.

adults.question: What does Paul say he was ordained as?
adults.option_a: A preacher, and an apostle, and a teacher of the Gentiles in faith and verity
adults.option_b: A priest of the temple in Jerusalem
adults.option_c: A judge over the twelve tribes
adults.option_d: A governor of the churches
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 2:7 — "Whereunto I am ordained a preacher, and an apostle, (I speak the truth in Christ, and lie not;) a teacher of the Gentiles in faith and verity."

adults.question: What does Paul say about Adam and Eve regarding the order of creation?
adults.option_a: Adam was first formed, then Eve
adults.option_b: Eve was formed first, then Adam
adults.option_c: They were formed at the same time
adults.option_d: The order of creation is not mentioned
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 2:13 — "For Adam was first formed, then Eve."

adults.question: Who was in the transgression according to verse 14?
adults.option_a: Adam was not deceived, but the woman being deceived was in the transgression
adults.option_b: Adam was deceived first
adults.option_c: Both were equally deceived
adults.option_d: Neither was truly deceived
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 2:14 — "And Adam was not deceived, but the woman being deceived was in the transgression."

adults.question: What does Paul say about women being saved through childbearing?
adults.option_a: She shall be saved in childbearing, if they continue in faith and charity and holiness with sobriety
adults.option_b: Childbearing is the means of spiritual salvation
adults.option_c: Women are exempt from salvation requirements
adults.option_d: Childbearing is forbidden for godly women
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 2:15 — "Notwithstanding she shall be saved in childbearing, if they continue in faith and charity and holiness with sobriety."

adults.question: What does Paul emphasize by saying "I speak the truth in Christ, and lie not" in verse 7?
adults.option_a: He solemnly affirms the truth of his apostolic appointment, especially regarding his mission to the Gentiles
adults.option_b: He is making a casual remark
adults.option_c: He is quoting an Old Testament prophecy
adults.option_d: He is defending himself against a specific false charge
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 2:7 — "I speak the truth in Christ, and lie not." Paul uses this emphatic assertion to validate his divine appointment as apostle to the Gentiles.

kids.question: What kind of person should a bishop (church leader) be?
kids.explanation: 1 Timothy 3:2 — "A bishop then must be blameless, the husband of one wife, vigilant, sober, of good behaviour."

kids.question: What kind of people should deacons (church helpers) be?
kids.explanation: 1 Timothy 3:8 — "Likewise must the deacons be grave, not doubletongued, not given to much wine, not greedy of filthy lucre."

kids.question: What does Paul call the church?
kids.option_a: The pillar and ground of the truth
kids.explanation: 1 Timothy 3:15 — "The church of the living God, the pillar and ground of the truth."

teens.question: What does Paul say about desiring the office of a bishop?
teens.option_a: He desireth a good work — it is a true saying
teens.option_b: It is prideful and should be avoided
teens.option_c: Only apostles should seek this office
teens.option_d: It is reserved for the wealthy
teens.explanation: 1 Timothy 3:1 — "This is a true saying, If a man desire the office of a bishop, he desireth a good work."

teens.question: Why must a bishop not be a novice (new convert)?
teens.option_a: Lest being lifted up with pride he fall into the condemnation of the devil
teens.option_b: Because new converts are too enthusiastic
teens.option_c: Because they have not paid enough dues
teens.option_d: Because they don't know enough people
teens.explanation: 1 Timothy 3:6 — "Not a novice, lest being lifted up with pride he fall into the condemnation of the devil."

teens.question: What must a bishop have from those outside the church?
teens.option_a: A good report of them which are without
teens.option_b: A letter of recommendation from the government
teens.option_c: Approval from the Jewish council
teens.option_d: Endorsement from other bishops
teens.explanation: 1 Timothy 3:7 — "Moreover he must have a good report of them which are without; lest he fall into reproach and the snare of the devil."

teens.question: What must deacons be proved (tested) before doing?
teens.option_a: Let them first be proved; then let them use the office of a deacon, being found blameless
teens.option_b: They need no testing if they volunteer
teens.option_c: They only need to pass a written exam
teens.option_d: They must serve for ten years first
teens.explanation: 1 Timothy 3:10 — "And let these also first be proved; then let them use the office of a deacon, being found blameless."

teens.question: What great mystery does Paul mention at the end of the chapter?
teens.option_a: Great is the mystery of godliness: God was manifest in the flesh
teens.option_b: The mystery of why evil exists
teens.option_c: The mystery of the end times
teens.option_d: The mystery of speaking in tongues
teens.explanation: 1 Timothy 3:16 — "And without controversy great is the mystery of godliness: God was manifest in the flesh."

adults.question: What does Paul say a bishop must be the husband of?
adults.option_a: One wife — "mias gunaikos andra" meaning a one-woman man
adults.option_b: No wife — he must be celibate
adults.option_c: Multiple wives to show he can manage a household
adults.option_d: A wife from a priestly family
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 3:2 — "A bishop then must be blameless, the husband of one wife."

adults.question: What is the connection Paul draws between managing a household and leading the church?
adults.option_a: If a man know not how to rule his own house, how shall he take care of the church of God?
adults.option_b: Managing a household is irrelevant to church leadership
adults.option_c: Only unmarried men can focus properly on the church
adults.option_d: Church leadership requires different skills than family leadership
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 3:5 — "For if a man know not how to rule his own house, how shall he take care of the church of God?"

adults.question: What must deacons hold with a pure conscience?
adults.option_a: The mystery of the faith
adults.option_b: The keys of the kingdom
adults.option_c: The scrolls of the law
adults.option_d: The traditions of the elders
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 3:9 — "Holding the mystery of the faith in a pure conscience."

adults.question: What does Paul say about the wives (of deacons or deaconesses) in verse 11?
adults.option_a: They must be grave, not slanderers, sober, faithful in all things
adults.option_b: They should remain silent at all times
adults.option_c: They should manage the church finances
adults.option_d: They should teach all the women's classes
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 3:11 — "Even so must their wives be grave, not slanderers, sober, faithful in all things."

adults.question: What do deacons who serve well purchase for themselves?
adults.option_a: A good degree and great boldness in the faith which is in Christ Jesus
adults.option_b: A promotion to the office of bishop
adults.option_c: A financial reward from the church
adults.option_d: A guaranteed place in heaven
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 3:13 — "For they that have used the office of a deacon well purchase to themselves a good degree, and great boldness in the faith which is in Christ Jesus."

adults.question: Why is Paul writing these instructions to Timothy?
adults.option_a: That thou mayest know how thou oughtest to behave thyself in the house of God
adults.option_b: To establish Paul's authority over Timothy
adults.option_c: To create a legal document for the church
adults.option_d: To record the history of the church at Ephesus
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 3:15 — "But if I tarry long, that thou mayest know how thou oughtest to behave thyself in the house of God."

adults.question: What six-part creedal statement does Paul give about the mystery of godliness?
adults.option_a: God was manifest in the flesh, justified in the Spirit, seen of angels, preached unto the Gentiles, believed on in the world, received up into glory
adults.option_b: Born of a virgin, baptized in Jordan, tempted in the wilderness, crucified on Calvary, buried in a tomb, risen on the third day
adults.option_c: Created the world, called Abraham, delivered Israel, gave the law, sent the prophets, established the church
adults.option_d: Father almighty, Son eternal, Spirit holy, church universal, saints forgiven, life everlasting
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 3:16 — "God was manifest in the flesh, justified in the Spirit, seen of angels, preached unto the Gentiles, believed on in the world, received up into glory."

adults.question: What does Paul call the church of the living God?
adults.option_a: The pillar and ground of the truth
adults.option_b: The bride of Christ
adults.option_c: The body of believers
adults.option_d: The temple of the Holy Ghost
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 3:15 — "The church of the living God, the pillar and ground of the truth."

kids.question: What does Paul say some people will do in the last times?
kids.explanation: 1 Timothy 4:1 — "Now the Spirit speaketh expressly, that in the latter times some shall depart from the faith."

kids.question: What does Paul tell Timothy to be for other believers?
kids.explanation: 1 Timothy 4:12 — "Be thou an example of the believers, in word, in conversation, in charity, in spirit, in faith, in purity."

kids.question: What is better than exercising your body?
kids.explanation: 1 Timothy 4:8 — "For bodily exercise profiteth little: but godliness is profitable unto all things."

teens.question: What will the departing ones give heed to?
teens.option_a: Seducing spirits and doctrines of devils
teens.option_b: The teachings of the apostles
teens.option_c: The wisdom of the prophets
teens.option_d: The commandments of God
teens.explanation: 1 Timothy 4:1 — "Giving heed to seducing spirits, and doctrines of devils."

teens.question: What two things do the false teachers forbid?
teens.option_a: Forbidding to marry and commanding to abstain from meats
teens.option_b: Teaching the gospel and baptizing believers
teens.option_c: Praying and giving thanks
teens.option_d: Reading scripture and singing hymns
teens.explanation: 1 Timothy 4:3 — "Forbidding to marry, and commanding to abstain from meats, which God hath created to be received with thanksgiving."

teens.question: What does Paul say about bodily exercise?
teens.option_a: Bodily exercise profiteth little, but godliness is profitable unto all things
teens.option_b: Bodily exercise is sinful
teens.option_c: Bodily exercise is the most important thing
teens.option_d: Bodily exercise and godliness are equal
teens.explanation: 1 Timothy 4:8 — "For bodily exercise profiteth little: but godliness is profitable unto all things, having promise of the life that now is, and of that which is to come."

teens.question: What does Paul tell Timothy about his youth?
teens.option_a: Let no man despise thy youth, but be thou an example of the believers
teens.option_b: Wait until you are older to teach
teens.option_c: Young people should stay silent in church
teens.option_d: Youth disqualifies you from leadership
teens.explanation: 1 Timothy 4:12 — "Let no man despise thy youth; but be thou an example of the believers, in word, in conversation, in charity, in spirit, in faith, in purity."

teens.question: What should Timothy give attendance to until Paul comes?
teens.option_a: Reading, exhortation, and doctrine
teens.option_b: Building construction and repairs
teens.option_c: Collecting donations and tithes
teens.option_d: Organizing feasts and celebrations
teens.explanation: 1 Timothy 4:13 — "Till I come, give attendance to reading, to exhortation, to doctrine."

adults.question: How does Paul describe the conscience of the false teachers?
adults.option_a: Speaking lies in hypocrisy, having their conscience seared with a hot iron
adults.option_b: Having a tender and sensitive conscience
adults.option_c: Having a well-informed conscience
adults.option_d: Having a conscience guided by Scripture
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 4:2 — "Speaking lies in hypocrisy; having their conscience seared with a hot iron."

adults.question: How is every creature of God sanctified?
adults.option_a: By the word of God and prayer
adults.option_b: By ritual cleansing and sacrifice
adults.option_c: By the approval of the elders
adults.option_d: By abstaining from it for a period
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 4:4-5 — "For every creature of God is good, and nothing to be refused, if it be received with thanksgiving: For it is sanctified by the word of God and prayer."

adults.question: What does Paul call Timothy if he puts these things in remembrance?
adults.option_a: A good minister of Jesus Christ, nourished up in the words of faith and of good doctrine
adults.option_b: A great apostle equal to Paul
adults.option_c: A worthy priest of the temple
adults.option_d: A mighty prophet of God
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 4:6 — "If thou put the brethren in remembrance of these things, thou shalt be a good minister of Jesus Christ, nourished up in the words of faith and of good doctrine, whereunto thou hast attained."

adults.question: What should Timothy refuse?
adults.option_a: Profane and old wives' fables, and exercise himself rather unto godliness
adults.option_b: All social gatherings and celebrations
adults.option_c: All contact with Gentile believers
adults.option_d: All food that has been offered to idols
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 4:7 — "But refuse profane and old wives' fables, and exercise thyself rather unto godliness."

adults.question: Why do believers labour and suffer reproach?
adults.option_a: Because we trust in the living God, who is the Saviour of all men, specially of those that believe
adults.option_b: Because suffering earns heavenly rewards
adults.option_c: Because it is punishment for past sins
adults.option_d: Because all religious people must suffer
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 4:10 — "For therefore we both labour and suffer reproach, because we trust in the living God, who is the Saviour of all men, specially of those that believe."

adults.question: What gift did Timothy receive by prophecy with the laying on of hands?
adults.option_a: A spiritual gift given by prophecy, with the laying on of the hands of the presbytery
adults.option_b: The ability to speak in tongues
adults.option_c: The power to heal the sick
adults.option_d: The authority to write Scripture
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 4:14 — "Neglect not the gift that is in thee, which was given thee by prophecy, with the laying on of the hands of the presbytery."

adults.question: What does Paul tell Timothy to meditate upon and give himself wholly to?
adults.option_a: These things — that his profiting may appear to all
adults.option_b: Ancient philosophical texts
adults.option_c: Military strategy and tactics
adults.option_d: The traditions of the Pharisees
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 4:15 — "Meditate upon these things; give thyself wholly to them; that thy profiting may appear to all."

adults.question: What does Paul say Timothy will save by taking heed to himself and the doctrine?
adults.option_a: Both himself and them that hear him
adults.option_b: Only himself
adults.option_c: Only those who are already believers
adults.option_d: Only the elders of the church
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 4:16 — "Take heed unto thyself, and unto the doctrine; continue in them: for in doing this thou shalt both save thyself, and them that hear thee."

kids.question: Who does Paul say the church should take care of?
kids.option_a: Widows who are truly alone and in need
kids.explanation: 1 Timothy 5:3 — "Honour widows that are widows indeed."

kids.question: What should children and grandchildren do for their family first?
kids.option_a: Show love at home and repay their parents
kids.option_b: Leave home as soon as possible
kids.option_c: Only help people outside the family
kids.option_d: Wait for the church to help their parents
kids.explanation: 1 Timothy 5:4 — "Let them learn first to shew piety at home, and to requite their parents: for that is good and acceptable before God."

kids.question: What does Paul say elders who lead well deserve?
kids.explanation: 1 Timothy 5:17 — "Let the elders that rule well be counted worthy of double honour."

teens.question: What does Paul say about someone who does not provide for his own family?
teens.option_a: He hath denied the faith and is worse than an infidel
teens.option_b: He is still a good believer
teens.option_c: He is showing trust in God
teens.option_d: He is focusing on spiritual things
teens.explanation: 1 Timothy 5:8 — "But if any provide not for his own, and specially for those of his own house, he hath denied the faith, and is worse than an infidel."

teens.question: What age must a widow be to be enrolled in the church's care?
teens.option_a: Not under threescore years old (sixty)
teens.option_b: At least thirty years old
teens.option_c: At least forty years old
teens.option_d: Any age if she is a widow
teens.explanation: 1 Timothy 5:9 — "Let not a widow be taken into the number under threescore years old."

teens.question: What does Paul say about elders who labour in the word and doctrine?
teens.option_a: They are worthy of double honour, especially those who labour in the word and doctrine
teens.option_b: They should receive the same as everyone else
teens.option_c: They need no special recognition
teens.option_d: They should volunteer without any support
teens.explanation: 1 Timothy 5:17 — "Let the elders that rule well be counted worthy of double honour, especially they who labour in the word and doctrine."

teens.question: What scripture does Paul quote about not muzzling the ox?
teens.option_a: Thou shalt not muzzle the ox that treadeth out the corn, and the labourer is worthy of his reward
teens.option_b: The ox knows his owner
teens.option_c: The ox is a clean animal for sacrifice
teens.option_d: The ox shall rest on the Sabbath
teens.explanation: 1 Timothy 5:18 — "For the scripture saith, Thou shalt not muzzle the ox that treadeth out the corn. And, The labourer is worthy of his reward."

teens.question: What does Paul tell Timothy about laying hands on people hastily?
teens.option_a: Lay hands suddenly on no man, neither be partaker of other men's sins
teens.option_b: Lay hands on everyone who asks
teens.option_c: Only lay hands on the wealthy
teens.option_d: Lay hands only on family members
teens.explanation: 1 Timothy 5:22 — "Lay hands suddenly on no man, neither be partaker of other men's sins: keep thyself pure."

adults.question: What does Paul say a widow indeed is?
adults.option_a: One who is desolate, trusteth in God, and continueth in supplications and prayers night and day
adults.option_b: Any woman whose husband has died
adults.option_c: Only a widow with no children
adults.option_d: A widow who remarries quickly
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 5:5 — "Now she that is a widow indeed, and desolate, trusteth in God, and continueth in supplications and prayers night and day."

adults.question: What does Paul say about the widow who lives in pleasure?
adults.option_a: She is dead while she liveth
adults.option_b: She is enjoying God's blessings
adults.option_c: She is living her best life
adults.option_d: She is setting a good example
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 5:6 — "But she that liveth in pleasure is dead while she liveth."

adults.question: What does Paul say about younger widows and their first faith?
adults.option_a: When they have begun to wax wanton against Christ, they will marry, having damnation because they have cast off their first faith
adults.option_b: They should never remarry under any circumstances
adults.option_c: They should immediately seek new husbands
adults.option_d: Their first faith is always permanent
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 5:11-12 — "But the younger widows refuse... they will marry; Having damnation, because they have cast off their first faith."

adults.question: What does Paul prefer younger widows to do?
adults.option_a: Marry, bear children, guide the house, and give none occasion to the adversary to speak reproachfully
adults.option_b: Remain single and devote themselves entirely to prayer
adults.option_c: Travel as missionaries to other churches
adults.option_d: Move back to their parents' households
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 5:14 — "I will therefore that the younger women marry, bear children, guide the house, give none occasion to the adversary to speak reproachfully."

adults.question: How should an accusation against an elder be received?
adults.option_a: Not except before two or three witnesses
adults.option_b: From any single accuser
adults.option_c: Only from other elders
adults.option_d: Only from the apostle himself
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 5:19 — "Against an elder receive not an accusation, but before two or three witnesses."

adults.question: What should be done with elders who sin?
adults.option_a: Rebuke them before all, that others also may fear
adults.option_b: Speak to them privately and never mention it again
adults.option_c: Ignore the sin to preserve church unity
adults.option_d: Immediately remove them without discussion
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 5:20 — "Them that sin rebuke before all, that others also may fear."

adults.question: What does Paul charge Timothy before God and the elect angels?
adults.option_a: Observe these things without preferring one before another, doing nothing by partiality
adults.option_b: Build a new meeting place for the church
adults.option_c: Appoint as many elders as possible
adults.option_d: Send missionaries to every Roman province
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 5:21 — "I charge thee before God, and the Lord Jesus Christ, and the elect angels, that thou observe these things without preferring one before another, doing nothing by partiality."

adults.question: What does Paul advise Timothy about his frequent infirmities?
adults.option_a: Drink no longer water, but use a little wine for thy stomach's sake and thine often infirmities
adults.option_b: Fast and pray until healed
adults.option_c: See a Greek physician immediately
adults.option_d: Accept suffering as God's will without remedy
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 5:23 — "Drink no longer water, but use a little wine for thy stomach's sake and thine often infirmities."

kids.question: What does Paul say is great gain?
kids.explanation: 1 Timothy 6:6 — "But godliness with contentment is great gain."

kids.question: What does Paul say about the love of money?
kids.option_a: It is the root of all evil
kids.option_b: It is the best thing in life
kids.option_d: It is a sign of God's blessing
kids.explanation: 1 Timothy 6:10 — "For the love of money is the root of all evil."

kids.question: What good fight does Paul tell Timothy to fight?
kids.option_b: A fight with swords and shields
kids.explanation: 1 Timothy 6:12 — "Fight the good fight of faith."

teens.question: What did we bring into this world and what can we carry out?
teens.option_a: We brought nothing in, and it is certain we can carry nothing out
teens.option_b: We brought our talents and will take our achievements
teens.option_c: We brought nothing but will carry out our memories
teens.option_d: We brought our souls and will carry out our rewards
teens.explanation: 1 Timothy 6:7 — "For we brought nothing into this world, and it is certain we can carry nothing out."

teens.question: What happens to people who want to be rich?
teens.option_a: They fall into temptation and a snare and many foolish and hurtful lusts that drown men in destruction and perdition
teens.option_b: They always succeed and are blessed by God
teens.option_c: They become generous and charitable
teens.option_d: They find true happiness and peace
teens.explanation: 1 Timothy 6:9 — "But they that will be rich fall into temptation and a snare, and into many foolish and hurtful lusts, which drown men in destruction and perdition."

teens.question: What should Timothy flee from and what should he follow after?
teens.option_a: Flee the love of money; follow after righteousness, godliness, faith, love, patience, meekness
teens.option_b: Flee all people; follow only the apostles
teens.option_c: Flee all cities; follow the wilderness path
teens.option_d: Flee all knowledge; follow only feelings
teens.explanation: 1 Timothy 6:11 — "But thou, O man of God, flee these things; and follow after righteousness, godliness, faith, love, patience, meekness."

teens.question: What good confession did Christ witness before Pontius Pilate?
teens.option_a: A good confession — Timothy should keep the commandment without spot until the appearing of the Lord Jesus Christ
teens.option_b: That he was the King of the Jews
teens.option_c: That he came to destroy Rome
teens.option_d: That he would return in three days
teens.explanation: 1 Timothy 6:13 — "I give thee charge in the sight of God... and before Christ Jesus, who before Pontius Pilate witnessed a good confession."

teens.question: What should the rich in this world NOT be?
teens.option_a: Highminded, nor trust in uncertain riches, but in the living God who giveth us richly all things to enjoy
teens.option_b: Generous with their money
teens.option_c: Involved in church activities
teens.option_d: Friends with poor people
teens.explanation: 1 Timothy 6:17 — "Charge them that are rich in this world, that they be not highminded, nor trust in uncertain riches, but in the living God, who giveth us richly all things to enjoy."

adults.question: What does Paul say about servants and their masters in verse 1?
adults.option_a: Servants should count their own masters worthy of all honour, that the name of God and his doctrine be not blasphemed
adults.option_b: Servants should demand their freedom in Christ
adults.option_c: Servants have no obligations to unbelieving masters
adults.option_d: Masters and servants are equal in all social matters
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 6:1 — "Let as many servants as are under the yoke count their own masters worthy of all honour, that the name of God and his doctrine be not blasphemed."

adults.question: What does Paul say about those who teach differently and do not consent to sound words?
adults.option_a: He is proud, knowing nothing, doting about questions and strifes of words, whereof cometh envy, strife, railings, evil surmisings
adults.option_b: He is a mature believer with a different perspective
adults.option_c: He is being led by a different spiritual gift
adults.option_d: He should be given more time to come to the truth
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 6:3-4 — "If any man teach otherwise, and consent not to wholesome words, even the words of our Lord Jesus Christ, and to the doctrine which is according to godliness; He is proud, knowing nothing."

adults.question: What do some suppose gain is?
adults.option_a: Godliness — thinking religion is a means of financial gain; from such withdraw thyself
adults.option_b: A fair and legitimate business practice
adults.option_c: A sign of God's favour and blessing
adults.option_d: The natural result of hard work
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 6:5 — "Perverse disputings of men of corrupt minds, and destitute of the truth, supposing that gain is godliness: from such withdraw thyself."

adults.question: What did some who coveted after money do?
adults.option_a: They erred from the faith and pierced themselves through with many sorrows
adults.option_b: They built large and prosperous churches
adults.option_c: They funded many missionary trips
adults.option_d: They became the most respected church leaders
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 6:10 — "Which while some coveted after, they have erred from the faith, and pierced themselves through with many sorrows."

adults.question: What titles does Paul give God in verses 15-16?
adults.option_a: The blessed and only Potentate, the King of kings, and Lord of lords, who only hath immortality, dwelling in the light which no man can approach unto
adults.option_b: The Ancient of Days, the Judge of all the earth
adults.option_c: The God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob
adults.option_d: The Lord of hosts, the Maker of heaven and earth
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 6:15-16 — "The blessed and only Potentate, the King of kings, and Lord of lords; Who only hath immortality, dwelling in the light which no man can approach unto."

adults.question: What does Paul tell the rich to be rich in?
adults.option_a: Good works, ready to distribute, willing to communicate
adults.option_b: Gold, silver, and precious stones
adults.option_c: Land, houses, and possessions
adults.option_d: Theological knowledge and debate skills
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 6:18 — "That they do good, that they be rich in good works, ready to distribute, willing to communicate."

adults.question: What does Paul tell Timothy to keep and avoid?
adults.option_a: Keep that which is committed to thy trust, avoiding profane and vain babblings, and oppositions of science falsely so called
adults.option_b: Keep the Jewish traditions and avoid Gentile customs
adults.option_c: Keep the peace and avoid all confrontation
adults.option_d: Keep his wealth and avoid poverty
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 6:20 — "O Timothy, keep that which is committed to thy trust, avoiding profane and vain babblings, and oppositions of science falsely so called."

adults.question: What happened to some who professed "science falsely so called"?
adults.option_a: They erred concerning the faith
adults.option_b: They became great scholars
adults.option_c: They discovered important truths
adults.option_d: They were promoted in the church
adults.explanation: 1 Timothy 6:21 — "Which some professing have erred concerning the faith. Grace be with thee. Amen."
